---
title: "1.12 Objects: Instances of Classes"
subtitle: "Part 4: Inheritance and Draw 7"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Class Hierarchy and Inheritance

### Vocabulary only in this lesson

Another important concept in object-oriented programming is **inheritance** (继承).

Although the AP CSA exam no longer covers inheritance, you should know what it means.

**Inheritance** is a way to create a new class that is based on an existing class.

## Class Hierarchy and Inheritance

### Superclass and subclass

The new class, called a **subclass**, inherits the attributes and behaviors of the existing class, called a **superclass**.

In Java, all classes are subclasses of a superclass called **Object**.

![Inheritance hierarchy for Pet superclass](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/inheritance-hierarchy-pet.png){fig-align="center" width="58%"}

## Turtle Methods

### Class diagram

Let's practice more with the `Turtle` class and its methods.

Here is a class diagram that shows some of the attributes and methods in the class `Turtle`.

![Turtle class diagram](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/turtleUMLClassDiagram.png){fig-align="center" width="52%"}

## Turtle World Coordinates

### Top-left origin

The Turtle world does not use the Cartesian coordinate system with `(0,0)` in the middle of the screen.

Instead, `(0,0)` is at the top left corner of the screen.

`x` increases to the right and `y` increases towards the bottom of the screen.

![Turtle world coordinates](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/coords.png){fig-align="center" width="34%"}

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: ParsonsDraw7`

The following code uses a turtle to draw the digital number `7` with just straight lines, but the code is mixed up.

Put the blocks in the correct order to first draw the line going up, towards the top of the page, and then turn and draw a line to the left to make a `7`.

Remember that the turtle is facing the top of the page when it is first created.

## Mixed Blocks

### Reorder task

```java
public class Draw7
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(300,300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turnLeft();
        yertle.forward();
        habitat.show(true);
    } // end main
} // end class
```

## Correct Order

### Draw a digital `7`

```java
public class Draw7
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(300,300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turnLeft();
        yertle.forward();
        habitat.show(true);
    } // end main
} // end class
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: TurtleDrawSeven`

After you put the mixed up code in order, type in the same code below to make the turtle draw a `7`.

Can you make `yertle` draw a `7` by going forward to go up and then left?

## Starter Code

### `TurtleDrawSeven`

```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleDraw7
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
        // TODO: Make yertle draw a 7 using the code above.

        habitat.show(true);
    }
}
```

## Test Requirement

### Preserved Runestone check

Runestone checks that the code contains this sequence:

```java
yertle.forward();
yertle.turnLeft();
yertle.forward();
```

That sequence draws the line up, turns left, and draws the top of the digital `7`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define inheritance as a subclass based on a superclass
- remember that all Java classes are subclasses of `Object`
- identify `(0,0)` at the top-left of the Turtle world
- order Turtle setup before movement calls
- use `forward`, `turnLeft`, `forward` to draw the `7`
- keep `habitat.show(true)` after the drawing commands
:::

## End

### 1.12 Part 4 complete

Part 5 continues with Draw 8 and the block-letter challenge.
